description.content:
Retrieve arrival and departure information of a vessel for a specific period of time.

Notes
  • Data is available for dates **after** 2015-01-16
  • Information about AIS-transmitted data
  • **MOVE_TYPE** values: 1=Departure, 0=Arrival
  • The frequency of allowed API calls is specific to your API key and is detailed in your contract as a number of successful calls per time period. For example “2 calls per minute”.
    Regardless of this agreed limit, each API key is technically restricted to a maximum of 100 total (including successful and unsuccessful) requests per minute to ensure system stability.

query.description: (Required) Version of the service to be executed. Use version 6 to get the latest

(Required) A uniquely assigned ID by MarineTraffic for the subject vessel

You can instead use imo or mmsi
The Maritime Mobile Service Identity (MMSI) of the vessel you wish to track
The International Maritime Organization (IMO) number of the vessel you wish to track
The maximum age, in minutes, of the returned port calls. Maximum value is 2880
Use 0 to only receive arrivals or 1 to only receive departures. If not used, the response will include both
Use 1 to exclude vessels in transit
Portcalls between fromdate and todate. Maximum period requested 190 days </br></br> Date format: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM
Portcalls between fromdate and todate. Maximum period requested 190 days </br></br> Date format: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM
Data filter: minimum DWT </br></br> Applicable to IMO-having vessels
Data filter: maximum DWT </br></br> Applicable to IMO-having vessels
Data filter: minimum GT </br></br> Applicable to IMO-having vessels
Data filter: maximum GT </br></br> Applicable to IMO-having vessels
Define market(s) for which you would like to receive port calls (single or comma separated values) - more

Resolution of the response. Available values:
  • simple
  • extended
If used with the value extended, the response includes voyage related data since the previous port call

Response type. Use one of the following:
  • xml
  • csv
  • jsono

description.content:
Get event based intelligence for a vessel.

Notes
  • Here is a COMPLETE LIST OF EVENT TYPES available for this endpoint. You can use these as values for the **event_type** query parameter.
  • Information about AIS-transmitted data
  • The frequency of allowed API calls is specific to your API key and is detailed in your contract as a number of successful calls per time period. For example “2 calls per minute”.
    Regardless of this agreed limit, each API key is technically restricted to a maximum of 100 total (including successful and unsuccessful) requests per minute to ensure system stability.

query.description: (Required) Version of the service to be executed. Use version 2 to get the latest

(Required) A uniquely assigned ID by MarineTraffic for the subject vessel

You can instead use imo or mmsi
The Maritime Mobile Service Identity (MMSI) of the vessel you wish to track
The International Maritime Organization (IMO) number of the vessel you wish to track
The maximum age, in minutes, of the returned port calls. Maximum value is 2880
Vessel Events between fromdate and todate </br></br> Date format: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS
Vessel Events between fromdate and todate </br></br> Date format: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS
You may use one or more event ids in comma separated format for the events you wish to receive
Limit the number of results

Response type. Use one of the following:
  • xml
  • csv
  • jsono

description.content:
Get berth arrival and departure information for a specific vessel.

Notes
  • Data is available for dates **after** 2017-07-03
  • Information about AIS-transmitted data
  • More information about input parameters: MARKET, SHIPCLASS
  • The frequency of allowed API calls is specific to your API key and is detailed in your contract as a number of successful calls per time period. For example “2 calls per minute”.
    Regardless of this agreed limit, each API key is technically restricted to a maximum of 100 total (including successful and unsuccessful) requests per minute to ensure system stability.

query.description: (Required) Version of the service to be executed. Use version 3 to get the latest

(Required) A uniquely assigned ID by MarineTraffic for the subject vessel

You can instead use imo or mmsi
The Maritime Mobile Service Identity (MMSI) of the vessel you wish to track
The International Maritime Organization (IMO) number of the vessel you wish to track
The maximum age, in minutes, of the returned port calls. Maximum value is 2880
Return Berth Calls where DOCK_TIMESTAMP_UTC is equal to or greater than provided fromdate. Maximum period requested 15 days </br></br> Date format: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS.
Return Berth Calls where DOCK_TIMESTAMP_UTC is equal to or less than provided todate. Maximum period requested 15 days </br></br> Date format: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS

Define market for which you would like to receive berth calls - more
  • if undefined and ship-class is also undefined, then return all
  • if undefined and ship-class is defined, return based on ship-class
  • if defined and ship-class is undefined, return all ship-classes of the specific market
Define ship class of interest - more
Data filter: minimum DWT </br></br> Use it to filter by size Cargo and Tanker IMO-having vessels (shiptype = 7,8)
Data filter: maximum DWT </br></br> Use it to filter by size Cargo and Tanker IMO-having vessels (shiptype = 7,8)
Data filter: minimum GT </br></br> Applicable to IMO-having vessels
Data filter: maximum GT </br></br> Applicable to IMO-having vessels

Use 0 to include both, 1 to only receive dockings or 2 for only undockings within the defined period. If not used, only dockings are returned

Available only from version 2 of the service

Response type. Use one of the following:
  • xml
  • csv
  • jsono

Resolution of the response. Available values:
  • simple
  • extended
If used with the value extended, the response includes voyage related data since the previous port call

Notes

  • The frequency of allowed API calls is specific to your API key and is detailed in your contract as a number of successful calls per time period. For example “2 calls per minute”.
    Regardless of this agreed limit, each API key is technically restricted to a maximum of 100 total (including successful and unsuccessful) requests per minute to ensure system stability.

description.content:
Retrieve arrival and departure information at a specific port, berth or terminal, for a specific period of time.

Notes
  • Data is available for dates **after** 2017-07-03
  • Information about AIS-transmitted data
  • More information about input parameters: MARKET, SHIPCLASS
  • The frequency of allowed API calls is specific to your API key and is detailed in your contract as a number of successful calls per time period. For example “2 calls per minute”.
    Regardless of this agreed limit, each API key is technically restricted to a maximum of 100 total (including successful and unsuccessful) requests per minute to ensure system stability.

query.description: (Required) Version of the service to be executed. Use version 3 to get the latest

(Required) The MarineTraffic ID of the port you wish to monitor (found on the URL of the respective Port page)

You can instead use port_unlocode, berthid or terminalid
Port UN LOCODE (without gaps or spaces)
The MarineTraffic ID of the berth you wish to monitor (list to be provided upon request)
The MarineTraffic ID of the terminal you wish to monitor (list to be provided upon request)
The maximum age, in minutes, of the returned port calls. Maximum value is 2880
Return Berth Calls where DOCK_TIMESTAMP_UTC is equal to or greater than provided fromdate. Maximum period requested 15 days </br></br> Date format: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS.
Return Berth Calls where DOCK_TIMESTAMP_UTC is equal to or less than provided todate. Maximum period requested 15 days </br></br> Date format: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS

Define market for which you would like to receive berth calls - more
  • if undefined and ship-class is also undefined, then return all
  • if undefined and ship-class is defined, return based on ship-class
  • if defined and ship-class is undefined, return all ship-classes of the specific market
Define ship class of interest - more
Data filter: minimum DWT </br></br> Use it to filter by size Cargo and Tanker IMO-having vessels (shiptype = 7,8)
Data filter: maximum DWT </br></br> Use it to filter by size Cargo and Tanker IMO-having vessels (shiptype = 7,8)
Data filter: minimum GT </br></br> Applicable to IMO-having vessels
Data filter: maximum GT </br></br> Applicable to IMO-having vessels

Use 0 to include both, 1 to only receive dockings or 2 for only undockings within the defined period. If not used, only dockings are returned

Available only from version 2 of the service

Response type. Use one of the following:
  • xml
  • csv
  • jsono

Resolution of the response. Available values:
  • simple
  • extended
If used with the value extended, the response includes voyage related data since the previous port call

description.content:
Port calls, berth calls, and event timelines for single vessels and entire ports — surfacing every arrival, departure, and berth touch detected by the global AIS network.

Contact Support:
 Name: MarineTraffic
